mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative alpha.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in spending and housing fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicate supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not precede negative 20-day returns in late-cycle regimes, or the predictive accuracy drops below 45% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading below 1.5 percent year-over-year triggers expectations of aggressive monetary accommodation, causing rapid positive repricing in equities within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when inflation falls below the threshold, or the signal generates negative alpha in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates signals structural labor market deterioration that suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a cumulative information coefficient below zero across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDP growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp rebounds in industrial production following a contraction phase indicate supply chain normalization and inventory restocking, triggering short-term positive equity momentum within one week.
failure_condition: Industrial production rebounds do not generate positive 5-day returns in recovery regimes, or the strategy yields a negative cumulative return over any 3-year out-of-sample period.
